title: Lastrea patens
taxonomy: Lastrea patens is classified as a species of fern in the family Athyriaceae. This classification places it within the genus Lastrea, which is a group of ferns commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The family Athyriaceae is a group of ferns that are known for their distinctive fronds and are often found in shaded, humid environments. The classification of Lastrea patens is also based on its unique characteristics, such as its leaf shape and venation pattern. Further classification at the species level is necessary to fully understand the relationships between Lastrea patens and other related species.
uses: I can find information about Lastrea patens, also known as the 'Broad-leaved Lady Fern'. Lastrea patens is used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world, particularly in Asia. intro: Lastrea patens, also known as the Broad-leaved Fern or Cinnamon Fern, is a type of vascular plant belonging to the family Thelypteridaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Lastrea patens include sesquiterpenoids, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, Lastrea patens is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including coughs, colds, and digestive problems.
